title: couldn't create rootless container: "mounting storage for container ...  input/output error"
body:
### Issue Description

What a heisenbug!

I created this bugreport with all the info and tried to reproduce it one last time and study logs in journald... But suddenly the error disappeared, my container was created and everything worked normally.

What can I do in the future if I stumble into this 'input/output error'?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. pull the image (it's pretty big)
```
$ podman pull docker.io/atinoda/text-generation-webui:llama-cpu-nightly
```

The input/output error and `/home/tt/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/6d6faee72321d989698b7b9c24e5baa4cce03d2586f54173b80573d10676c2a6/merged: invalid argument` are highly suspicious though they don't exactly say what's wrong.

I studied the rootless troubleshooting guide and none of the advises for 'invalid argument' apply to me. I have been using this rootless setup for years without a problem.

### Describe the results you expected

Running container

### Podman in a container

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

comments.body:
The input/output error and /home/tt/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/6d6faee72321d989698b7b9c24e5baa4cce03d2586f54173b80573d10676c2a6/merged: invalid argument are highly suspicious though they don't exactly say what's wrong.

We really should display the mount options when we get a failure.

I've tried locally and I am not able to reproduce, could you run the following command and share the `output.log` file?

```
podman unshare strace -f -v -s 1000 -o output.log podman run --rm -ti docker.io/atinoda/text-generation-webui:llama-cpu-nightly
```

is there any useful information in the dmesg output?

Thanks for the guidance, @giuseppe and @rhatdan 

As I said in the original post, once I started debugging the issue, it went away and the container was created and started correctly.

If it ever happens again, I'll provide `strace` and dmesg logs. Thanks!
